attacking_style: Pure sprinter who launches in final 200m, thriving in disorganized/chaotic finales rather than structured leadout trains. Excels at late positioning decisions and wheel selection when sprints fragment.
team_tactics: Operates with minimal or absent leadout support, freelancing off rival trains. Team DSM provided little dedicated sprint infrastructure, forcing self-reliance and opportunistic positioning behind competitors' leadouts.
terrain_preference: Flat bunch sprints and technical finales where positioning trumps pure power. Vulnerable on even modest gradients before sprint finishes; struggles when climbs precede final kilometers despite occasional recovery resilience.
vulnerability: Positional awareness in structured sprints can fail catastrophically (finished 50th when favored). Dropped on summit finishes with modest climbing before sprints. Requires chaotic conditions to overcome lack of leadout.
key_patterns: Won 4+ sprints without dedicated leadout, freelancing off rival trains more effectively than supported sprinters
Thrives in chaos: multiple wins from disrupted sprints with crashes/late catches eliminating organized trains
Photo-finish specialist: won multiple tire-width margins, suggesting excellent bike-handling and timing in tight finishes
Early career positioning errors (50th when favored) evolved to tactical maturity by 2022-2023
Climbing vulnerability: dropped on modest gradients preceding sprints, limits stage-winning range to pure flat finales
form_trajectory: positive: Won 2023 Vuelta and Giro stages after years of development, left DSM after breakthrough results, suggesting confidence in established sprint competitiveness
built_from: 2021 La Vuelta ciclista a Espana Stage 8
2023 La Vuelta Ciclista a Espana Stage 19
2023 La Vuelta Ciclista a Espana Stage 19
